g.text: 1. Server bootstrap (connectionless — no accept, no boss/worker)
No codecs, no frame decoders — datagrams are self-delimiting.
A single EventLoopGroup handles both I/O and business logic;
because UDP has no accept() step.
2. Client bootstrap (bind ephemeral port to receive replies)
Client is also connectionless: bind(0) asks the OS for an
ephemeral port so the server has an address to reply to.
No connect() — writeAndFlush supplies the destination per packet.
channel active on ephemeral port P
3. Client -> Server : broadcast query (single packet, no handshake)
UDP broadcast delivered to every host
on the subnet whose socket is bound to :8080
// unicast back to sender, not broadcast
ignore // not our reply (broadcasts may carry other traffic)
If no matching reply arrives within 15 s
(packet lost, server down, or wrong subnet):
Note: UDP gives no delivery guarantee. A lost query or lost reply
is indistinguishable from a slow one without application-level
Closes the datagram channel — the server stops receiving on :8080.
Closes the client channel — closeFuture returns,
